report:
# What is retrieval-augmented generation (RAG)?

Retrieval-augmented generation is an architecture that retrieves relevant documents from an external corpus at query time and conditions a language model's generation on them [1]. It reduces hallucination by grounding answers in retrieved evidence rather than relying solely on the model's parameters [1].

corpus.text: Retrieval-augmented generation retrieves relevant documents from an external corpus at query time and conditions generation on them, grounding answers in retrieved evidence to reduce hallucination.
